title: Filing Claims for Other Policies (Auto, Home, Disability)
summary: Beyond life insurance, several other policies may need updating, canceling, or claiming — easy to overlook amid bigger tasks.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Filing Claims for Other Policies (Auto, Home, Disability) | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Beyond life insurance, several other policies may need updating, canceling, or claiming — easy to overlook amid bigger tasks.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Auto and home insurance need to be updated with a new named insured, not necessarily canceled.
Any pending disability claims typically end at death, though back payments owed may still apply.
Reviewing all policies together avoids missing one.

paragraphs: Auto and homeowners policies generally need to be updated to reflect a new named insured (such as a surviving spouse) rather than canceled outright, especially if the property or vehicle continues to be used.

If the person had a pending or ongoing disability claim, benefits generally stop at death, though any amount already owed for time before death may still be payable to the estate — worth confirming directly with the insurer rather than assuming it's simply closed.

nextSteps: Review all active policies together, not just life insurance.
Update auto and home policies with the correct named insured.
Ask about any amounts owed on a pending disability claim.
disclaimer: General information — each insurer can confirm the specific status and requirements for a given policy.

sources.name: NAIC: A Consumer's Guide to Auto Insurance
